stdout:
[36m[Codex][0m Hold for Codex...


[36m[Codex][0m Hi, I’m Codex. I can summarize what was discussed and turn decisions into concrete next steps, or quickly inspect project files and report the relevant answer.


[36m[Codex][0m Priya, the last person’s name I see spoken in the captions was “Priya.”


[36m[Codex][0m Last 5 minutes: the group focused on rollout risk and reliability checks. Main points were confirming the marker-file mechanism survives a crash, writing a fixture for the wizard re-import edge case, checking MCP startup latency and byte limits, watching Sentry again, and narrowing rollout to 10%.


[36m[Codex][0m Alice said, “let me share the dashboard link — Sentry alerts are firing on the auth path — Sentry alerts are firing on the auth path.” Carol said, “the Sentry alerts are firing again,” and Eve said, “let me check Sentry one more time.”


[36m[Codex][0m So far, the captioned speakers are Alice, Bob, Carol, Dan, and Eve. Mohammed and Priya were mentioned, but I don’t see them speaking in the captions.


[36m[Codex][0m About 8–10 minutes ago, the discussion was around test/database setup, caption issues, rollout risk, a leaked token, Mohammed’s database input, and Priya’s approval-policy concern. The clearest thread was reliability and launch readiness: database choice, token cleanup, and whether to narrow rollout to 10%.


[36m[Codex][0m Yes. Alice said, “I want to bring up the launch timeline,” and Dan said, “the launch is the priority this week.”


[36m[Codex][0m The meeting centered on launch readiness and reliability. Key issues were Sentry alerts on the auth path, revoking a leaked token, rollout risk with a suggested 10% rollout, the wizard re-import flow now being idempotent, and checking marker-file crash handling.


[36m[Codex][0m Other threads included the database split of Postgres for production and SQLite for tests, slipped migration timing, caption gaps, MCP startup latency, byte-limit checks, and Priya’s concern about the approval policy.


[36m[Codex][0m Bob said, “Postgres for prod SQLite for tests is the standard split.” Alice repeated the same line later, and also said, “Mohammed pinged me about the database pick.”
